title: Union Pacific stock surge would add more than about 120 points to the Dow transports
text: Shares of Union Pacific Corp. UNP soared 10.1% in premarket trading Friday, which by themselves would lead the Dow Jones Transportation Average DJT to outperform the broader stock market, after railroad operator said Chief Executive Lance Fritz would step down after eight years in the role. The announcement over the weekend came after urging by activist investor Soroban Capital Partners, which owns a stake of about $1.6 billion in Union Pacific.

title: Unlocking The Power Of Dividend Appreciation: The Story Of Union Pacific
text: UNP has a strong history of returning value to shareholders through consistent dividends and share buybacks, with a low price-to-earnings ratio of just 16x 2023 earnings per share. Union Pacific has a long-standing history of being one of the largest and most efficient railroads in North America, with a well-established network spanning 23 states and connecting major economic hubs.

title: Union Pacific on lookout for new CEO
text: Union Pacific confirmed Sunday that CEO Lance Fritz will step down sometime this year amid pressure from a shareholder. The CEO search became public after a request by UP shareholder Soroban Capital Partners, which had indicated that it wanted to move discussions about the CEO search to a public level, according to UP (NYSE: UNP).

title: Hedge fund wants to replace Union Pacific CEO
text: Hedge fund Soroban Capital Partners is pushing Union Pacific Corp. to replace Chief Executive Lance Fritz, arguing the railroad has underperformed on his watch, according to a letter the fund sent the railroad operator's board Sunday.

title: Union Pacific Miss on Earnings, Dividend Still Intact
